['SNAIC Knowledge Base

Document Metadata

Primary entity: SIT x NVIDIA AI Centre

Short name: SNAIC

Official SIT name: AI Centre (in collaboration with NVIDIA)

Organisation type: Strategic collaboration between Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and NVIDIA

Launch date: April 2024

Official opening: October 2025

Location: SIT@Punggol, Block E2, Level 7, Room E2-07-12

Campus area: Punggol Digital District

Country: Singapore

Canonical Description

SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between SIT and NVIDIA. It helps companies adopt AI through applied research, AI solution development, and talent development. It also gives SIT students and professionals hands-on experience with real-world AI projects.

One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.

 Mission

Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.

 Vision

Bring the potential of AI into business operations so organisations can improve efficiency, intelligence, creativity, and innovation.

 Core Problems SNAIC Helps Address

Problem 1: Technology barriers

Companies may face challenges related to data availability, cost, tooling, and technical complexity.

Problem 2: Computational resource barriers

Companies may lack access to suitable hardware, GPUs, or cloud compute.

Problem 3: Talent barriers

Companies may not have enough skilled, industry-ready AI talent.

 SNAIC Solution Areas

Solution 1: Enhance domain knowledge

SNAIC combines AI expertise with industry domain knowledge.

Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI

SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems.

Solution 3: Cost-efficient AI solutions

SNAIC develops practical AI solutions that are aligned to business value and resource constraints.

Industry Sectors Supported

Healthcare

Education

Logistics

Finance

Manufacturing

Telecommunication

Transportation

Public sector and infrastructure

 is already funded by IMDA. Other government subsidies should not be used because that would amount to double dipping.']
response:
SNAIC supports the Healthcare sector through co-developing practical AI solutions and upskilling talent. For hospitals and clinics, SNAIC offers AI projects such as LionNutriAI for precision nutrition assessment, Automated Visual Acuity Testing for eyesight testing automation, Enhancing Ultrasound Image Reconstruction using distributed continual learning, and AI Debator for structured healthcare debate. These are part of SNAIC’s work in LLMs, Healthcare AI, and Digital Biology.

SNAIC helps healthcare organisations by combining AI expertise with industry knowledge, integrating real-time data with Generative AI, and delivering cost-efficient AI solutions aligned to business value. Through its collaboration model, SNAIC works with SIT and NVIDIA experts to co-supervise projects, ensuring end-to-end AI translation and AI competency development for professionals. Healthcare companies can engage via Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, or upskilling initiatives to build AI talent internally.

For detailed support or to explore specific AI applications in healthcare, contacting SNAIC directly through their official website is recommended.

user_input: Dr Maya Lee ask how NVIDIA can help our manufacturing company with AI projects through SNAIC, like what AI talent and NVIDIA compute resources they actually contribute, because we want to upskill our staff and need to know what we get from them
reference: SNAIC, together with NVIDIA, supports your manufacturing AI projects by providing both AI talent and compute resources as part of the collaboration. This includes AI experts from SIT and NVIDIA, as well as postgraduate and undergraduate students who work directly with your company to co-develop and customise AI solutions while transferring knowledge to your team for upskilling. On the compute side, you gain access to NVIDIA DGX H200 systems for large-scale model training and data processing. These resources are jointly defined during the project setup phase, where both parties align on talent, technology, and funding, ensuring your team benefits from hands-on experience and capability building while delivering a practical AI solution.

user_input: What role does SIT play in the resource identification step of SNAIC's AI projects, including funding mechanisms and talent contributions?
reference: In the resource identification step, SIT helps work with the company to determine the required talent, technology, budget, and funding mechanism for the AI project. The possible funding routes include a company-funded project, a joint grant application by the company and SIT, an Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s project, the IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme, and an Integrative Team Project. On the talent side, SNAIC’s contribution may include AI talent and expertise from SIT and NVIDIA, along with postgraduate and undergraduate students, plus key AI technologies and NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources. Overall, SIT’s role is to help align resources, confirm contributions, and shape the project setup before development begins.

user_input: What does the SIT x NVIDIA AI Center do to help manufacturing companies adopt AI?
reference: The SIT x NVIDIA AI Centre (SNAIC) helps manufacturing companies adopt AI by conducting applied research, developing AI solutions, and providing talent development and hands‑on experience with real‑world AI projects. It addresses technology barriers, computational resource barriers, and talent barriers, enhances domain knowledge, integrates real‑time data with Generative AI, and creates cost‑efficient AI solutions aligned to business value and resource constraints.

It is positioned as a foundational capability that enables SNAIC to integrate real-time operational data with modern AI systems, directly supporting Solution 2 under SNAIC Solution Areas: "Integrate real-time data with Generative AI."

This positioning implies that RAG enhances the relevance and accuracy of AI-generated outputs by grounding them in up-to-date, domain-specific data—critical for practical AI solutions in industry settings. For AI solutions co-developed with partners like your manufacturing company, RAG allows for context-aware applications such as intelligent maintenance systems, real-time quality control assistants, or supply chain optimisation tools that leverage live sensor or operational data.

In talent development programmes, RAG is likely incorporated into upskilling and training initiatives as part of SNAIC’s focus on applied AI expertise. Industry professionals and students in Industrial Doctorates or Master’s programmes would gain hands-on experience implementing RAG pipelines, ensuring they are equipped to build and deploy AI systems that are both powerful and practically applicable to real-world industrial challenges. This strengthens the AI talent pipeline by aligning skills with SNAIC’s applied research and industry collaboration model.

['SNAIC Knowledge Base

Document Metadata

Primary entity: SIT x NVIDIA AI Centre

Short name: SNAIC

Official SIT name: AI Centre (in collaboration with NVIDIA)

Organisation type: Strategic collaboration between Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and NVIDIA

Launch date: April 2024

Official opening: October 2025

Location: SIT@Punggol, Block E2, Level 7, Room E2-07-12

Campus area: Punggol Digital District

Country: Singapore

Canonical Description

SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between SIT and NVIDIA. It helps companies adopt AI through applied research, AI solution development, and talent development. It also gives SIT students and professionals hands-on experience with real-world AI projects.

One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.

 Mission

Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.

 Vision

Bring the potential of AI into business operations so organisations can improve efficiency, intelligence, creativity, and innovation.

 Core Problems SNAIC Helps Address

Problem 1: Technology barriers

Companies may face challenges related to data availability, cost, tooling, and technical complexity.

Problem 2: Computational resource barriers

Companies may lack access to suitable hardware, GPUs, or cloud compute.

Problem 3: Talent barriers

Companies may not have enough skilled, industry-ready AI talent.

 SNAIC Solution Areas

Solution 1: Enhance domain knowledge

SNAIC combines AI expertise with industry domain knowledge.

Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI

SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems.

Solution 3: Cost-efficient AI solutions

SNAIC develops practical AI solutions that are aligned to business value and resource constraints.

Industry Sectors Supported

Healthcare

Education

Logistics

Finance

Manufacturing

Telecommunication

Transportation

Public sector and infrastructure

 is already funded by IMDA. Other government subsidies should not be used because that would amount to double dipping.']

SNAIC also focuses on domain areas including Industry and Manufacturing, and develops practical AI solutions for real use cases in this sector.
